word_identity.inference_note: Ambition is a morally neutral modern term that Scripture handles through two distinct concepts — selfish ambition (always negative) and godly zeal/aspiration (positive). The neutral modern sense is ABSENT. The combined list summary identified qana, gav, and zelos as conceptually related to ambition but these belong to their own separate entries. Zeal is handled as a separate word for analysis.

qualification_assessment.qualification_basis: eritheia (G2052) names a disposition of selfish self-seeking located explicitly in the heart (Jam 3:14) and operating as a motive behind external conduct (Phil 1:17, 2:3); filotimeomai (G5389) names an inner drive of honour-aspiration directed toward worthy goals. Together they name two poles of an inner motivational orientation, but neither term maps directly onto the morally neutral modern concept of ambition.
qualification_assessment.qualification_flag: Source list is Low Confidence / Inferred. The word is a conceptual inference category rather than a direct biblical term. No Hebrew terms appear in the STEP data for this word. The qualification rests entirely on two Greek terms.

term_inventory.meaning_summary: Selfish ambition, faction, strife; the service of a party or party spirit; feud, faction; contentious disposition, selfish ambition; by implication untowardness and disobedience. LSJ traces the root from labour for wages, through canvassing for public office and intriguing, to selfish or factious ambition.

term_inventory.anchor_grammatical_form: Verb Present Middle or Passive Deponent Participle Accusative Singular Masculine
term_inventory.meaning_summary: To have an ambition, aspire to a goal; primarily to be ambitious of honour; by implication to exert oneself to accomplish a thing, use one's utmost efforts, endeavour earnestly, aspire to a goal.

cross_registry_links.connecting_term: zelos — cited in LSJ for eritheia: zelos kai eritheia
cross_registry_links.note: The LSJ entry for eritheia explicitly pairs it with zelos (zelos kai eritheia) as a NT usage; zelos is a separate word entry in the 181 and will carry the motivational energy dimension of ambition that eritheia approaches from the negative side.
timē (honour) is a component root of filotimeomai (philo + timē = love of honour) and is listed as a related word; if honour is a separate entry in the 181 the root connection is direct.

erethizō is the sole related word under eritheia, sharing its root; the connection between selfish ambition and provocation/irritation as root-related concepts may be relevant if provocation is a separate entry in the 181.

filautos (self-loving, selfish) appears in the filotimeomai related words cluster and names the inner-being disposition of self-orientation that eritheia expresses in practice; may correspond to a separate entry in the 181.
filarguria belongs to the same philo- motivational family as filotimeomai and names a specific misdirected love-aspiration; may correspond to a separate entry in the 181.

data_quality_flags.description: No Hebrew terms appear in the STEP related word list for ambition. The combined list primary term assessment named qana (H7065) and gav as relevant Hebrew terms, but per the source file note these belong to separate word entries. No Hebrew analysis is possible for this word from the available STEP data.

The combined list primary term assessment named zelos (G2205) as the motivational energy dimension of ambition. Per the source file note, zelos is a separate word entry in the 181. The LSJ entry for eritheia cites the pairing zelos kai eritheia — this is recorded in cross-registry links. Beyond that note, zelos data belongs to its own Phase 1 record.

Source list classification is Low Confidence / Inferred. All findings from this Phase 1 record carry lower confidence than High Confidence or Missing Inner Being Words entries and should be weighted accordingly in Phase 2.
